title: Sai Speciality Centre | Maternity & Fertility Care Bangalore
heading: Leading Maternity & Child Health Center – 10+ Years of Excellence
description: Sai Speciality Centre is a trusted women's healthcare hospital in Bangalore for gynecology, fertility, pregnancy, maternity, and wellness care.

Experienced Doctor Quality & Safety Consultations Lab, Pharmacy & Ultrasound ✦ Meet Our Doctor Dr. Kavitha Lakshmi Easwaran Dr. Kavitha Lakshmi Easwaran is a Senior Consultant Obstetrician and Gynaecologist with 26 years of experience in women’s healthcare.Her expertise includes high-risk obstetrics, comprehensive gynaecological care, all gynaecological procedures including vaginal surgeries, and infertility management up to Intrauterine Insemination (IUI). Dr. Kavitha Lakshmi Easwaran is a Senior Consultant Obstetrician and Gynaecologist with 26 years of experience in women’s healthcare. Her expertise includes high-risk obstetrics, comprehensive gynaecological care, all gynaecological procedures including vaginal surgeries, and infertility management up to Intrauterine Insemination (IUI). Know More ✦ A Doctor’s Perspective Evidence-based guidance and real-world experience, helping you make better decisions about your health. ✦ Our Resources Trusted Resources for Your Well-Being May 21st, 2026 Many Indian women in their 30s and 40s notice unusual changes in their bodies and emotions, but often assume it is due to stress, busy schedules, or aging. However, these symptoms may actually be linked to perimenopause, a natural transition phase before menopause. Early awareness can help women take better care of their physical and emotional health. Read More May 21st, 2026 Many women in their late 30s and 40s notice sudden weight gain even when their eating habits have not changed much. Clothes may feel tighter, belly fat may increase, and losing weight can become more difficult than before. In many cases, this is linked to perimenopause. Read More May 21st, 2026 Many women experience constant tiredness, weight gain, hair fall, mood changes, or irregular periods without realizing that these symptoms may be linked to hypothyroidism. Because the signs develop slowly, they are often ignored or mistaken for stress, aging, or lifestyle problems. Read More May 21st, 2026 Many women struggle with stubborn belly fat even after following diets and exercise routines. In many cases, the problem may not only be calories or lack of activity — it may be linked to cortisol, the body’s main stress hormone. Read More May 21st, 2026 When couples face difficulty conceiving, fertility discussions often focus more on women. However, male fertility plays an equally important role in pregnancy. In many cases, fertility problems may be linked to sperm health, hormone imbalance, lifestyle habits, or underlying medical conditions in men. Read More May 21st, 2026 Preparing for embryo transfer is one of the most important stages in the IVF journey. Along with medications and medical care, nutrition also plays a major role in supporting implantation, hormone balance, and overall reproductive health. Read More May 21st, 2026 Vaginal itching and burning are common problems many women experience at some point in life. While yeast infections are often blamed first, they are not the only reason behind these uncomfortable symptoms. In many cases, irritation, hormonal imbalance, infections, allergies, or skin conditions may be the actual cause. Read More May 21st, 2026 Urinary tract infections (UTIs) are one of the most common health problems affecting women. While many women recover completely after treatment, some continue to experience repeated infections that return every few months. This condition is known as recurrent UTI. Read More May 21st, 2026 Pregnancy involves several important scans and health checkups that help monitor both the mother and the baby. One such important test is the cervical length scan, which helps doctors assess the risk of preterm labor and pregnancy complications. Read More May 21st, 2026 Menstrual hygiene is an important part of women’s health, yet many myths and misconceptions still exist even in modern cities like Bangalore. On Menstrual Hygiene Day, it is important to discuss the common myths women continue to believe and understand the facts behind safe menstrual care. Read More May 21st, 2026 World Infertility Awareness Month helps bring attention to the emotional, physical, and medical challenges faced by couples struggling to conceive. Infertility affects millions of couples worldwide, yet many people still hesitate to talk openly about it due to stress, stigma, and misinformation.
